items.q: Is a tennis serve a closed or open skill?
items.correct: Closed - performed in a predictable, self-paced environment.
items.misconception: Open - because tennis is a fast-paced sport.
items.why: Students judge by the sport, not the moment. The serve happens at the server's pace with a stationary ball - closed. The return of serve is open. Skill classification depends on the SPECIFIC action's environment.

items.q: Most movements in the human body use which class of lever?
items.why: Students assume the most familiar (first class - seesaw) is most common. Actually most body levers are third class (effort between fulcrum and load) - like the biceps curl. They sacrifice mechanical advantage for speed/range.

items.q: Which type of muscle is under voluntary control?
items.why: Students think all muscles can be controlled if we try hard enough. Cardiac and smooth muscle are involuntary - you can't consciously beat your heart. Only skeletal muscle responds to conscious commands.
Correct answer: From the heart. “To the heart” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: An artery is near skin. “Bones meet” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Typical resting heart rate (beats per minute, average)?
items.why: The real figure is 70. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
